maybe this is a hot take but i don't see it as orientalism when

1) it is strongly based on the actual history of nineveh, not some made up victorian nonsense
2) it is made clear that the aram people have returned to ancient traditions in an anomalous way and this is not "normal" for levantine people
3) ramona's bigotry is actively criticized and satirized by the plot... the problem with this is most people want to to see ramona as a flawless character when she isn't, or at least wasn't, she really could be extremely petty and unempathetic in arc 1 despite fundamentally being a good being, but that's a tough pill to swallow
